summary: AR-SAT Room is a design-and-schema handoff for an adversarially robust structured-analytic-technique room.
final_use_case: It matters because structured analytic techniques become more useful when their inputs, adversarial failure modes, interface logic, data model, and review obligations are built into the workspace.

instruments.input: source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime
instruments.operation: AR-SAT Room is a design-and-schema handoff for an adversarially robust structured-analytic-technique room.
instruments.output: bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct
instruments.status: Documented handoff or prototype; live status still to verify
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime and produce bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct.
instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through AR-SAT Room Handoff Identity; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.

instruments.input: user roles, task sequence, room state, and domain objects
instruments.operation: It routes an intelligence problem through a Room View that binds triad method, products, and proof; Idea Refinery, Patch Forge, baseline-comparison Evaluation Harness, JSON-Schema entities, command-palette behavior, station drawer, audit timeline, evidence handling, adversarial stress, and reviewer workflow make SAT use inspectable rather than decorative. The handoff names React/Next/Vite as the intended componentization path, while backend, auth, persistence, and vector search remain outside this pass.
instruments.output: room logic that turns the concept into a repeatable workflow
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires user roles, task sequence, room state, and domain objects

instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept user roles, task sequence, room state, and domain objects and produce room logic that turns the concept into a repeatable workflow.
instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through AR-SAT Room Room Task Model; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.

instruments.instrument_name: AR-SAT Room Data Schema And Persistence Plan
instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library.

instruments.input: project source text and the named component `Room View`
instruments.operation: treats `Room View` as a room or workbench component that gives the project a usable surface rather than only a written description. Source context: This pack is the v0.1 design + schema handoff for AR-SAT (Adversarially-Robust SATs) / CDIS v2, an intelligence-community workflow platform whose flagship screen is the "Room View" — a single cartographic page that presents, at a glance, the triad method — products — proof. The room is an auditable interdisciplinary Idea Refinery, a Patch Forge for deployable SAT work products, and a baseline-comparison Evaluation.
instruments.output: a room, workbench, or interface function tied to `Room View`
